title: vandellia subulata
intro: Vandellia subulata, also known as 'kutu babi' or 'rat's bane', is a type of medicinal plant native to tropical regions of Asia, particularly in the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The medicinal properties of Vandellia subulata include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. Vandellia subulata can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or topical paste, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues.
